name: Patient E
presenting_complaint: Right eye pain and watering since this morning after gardening; feels like there is something in the eye.
history: Hypertension (untreated, baseline SBP often 150-165). Otherwise independent. Walked himself in by private vehicle. NRS 2/10 foreign-body sensation, no visual loss. AVPU=A. No contact lens use.

trajectory.state: Alert, comfortable, mild watering of right eye. Vitals stable apart from chronic hypertension.
Topical anaesthetic + fluorescein staining shows linear corneal abrasion, no penetrating injury. Pain relieved.
Lubricating drops + chloramphenicol prescribed. Safety-net advice for worsening vision/pain. Discharged.

red_herrings: BP is high but is chronic, not acute
